title: Eria montana
taxonomy: Eria montana is classified as a genus of orchids in the family Orchidaceae. It is a type of flowering plant native to tropical regions. Eria montana is commonly found in the Himalayan region and parts of Southeast Asia. The plant's classification also places it in the subfamily Orchidoideae within the Orchidaceae family. Eria montana's specific classification is Eria montana (King & Pantl.) Schltr.
uses: Eria montana is a plant with a long history of traditional use in medicine, particularly in Southeast Asia. Traditionally, the plant's roots have been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin disorders. In addition, the plant has been used to treat respiratory issues such as coughs and colds. The medicinal properties of Eria montana are attributed to the presence of bioactive compounds, including alkaloids and glycosides. Further research is needed to fully understand the plant's potential medicinal applications.
